idea-plugin.name: SonarQube Jira Issuer
idea-plugin.description:
SonarQube Jira Issuer — Load SonarQube issues and push them to Jira as structured, batched tickets — directly from your IDE. No copy-pasting required.

Connect to any SonarQube instance (SonarCloud or self-managed), load issues by type and status, then either export ticket descriptions to a local file or create Jira tickets directly via the Jira REST API.

Key features:
  • Direct Jira creation — push tickets straight to Jira Cloud or Server/DC
  • Sprint selection — assign tickets to an active or future sprint, or leave in backlog
  • Dynamic custom fields — all optional Jira fields for the chosen project/issue-type are shown automatically; fill in any you need
  • Auto story-point detection — finds the correct story-points field for your Jira setup (Next-Gen or Classic) and sets it automatically from calculated estimates
  • Team deduplication — before creating, queries Jira by sq-<key> labels so two developers never create the same ticket twice
  • Secure token storage — SonarQube and Jira tokens stored in IntelliJ PasswordSafe (OS keychain / KeePass), never in plain text
  • Browse projects — discover accessible SonarQube projects directly from the IDE
  • Filter issues — by type (Bug, Code Smell, Vulnerability, Security Hotspot) and status
  • Smart batching — group issues into configurable Jira ticket batches
  • Automatic story-point calculation — effort estimated from issue data (1 SP = 1 working day), rounded to nearest 0.5
  • Epic link support — link created tickets to an existing Epic key
  • File export mode — Jira wiki-markup tables for copy-paste workflows
  • Incremental export — already-created tickets are never duplicated across runs (tracked via Jira labels and a local mapping file)
Supported Jira deployments:
  • Jira Cloud (*.atlassian.net) — API v3, ADF descriptions, Basic auth (email + API token)
  • Jira Server / Data Center — API v2, wiki-markup descriptions, Bearer PAT authentication
idea-plugin.change-notes:
4.0.0
  • New: Sprint selection — assign tickets to an active or future sprint, or leave in backlog
  • New: Dynamic custom fields editor — all optional Jira fields for the selected project/issue-type are loaded automatically; fill in any you need
  • New: Auto-detection of the story-points field by name (handles both Next-Gen customfield_10016 and Classic customfield_10028)
  • New: Team-safe deduplication via sq-<issueKey> Jira labels — queries Jira before creation so no two developers create the same ticket
  • Fix: SonarQube token now saved correctly when using the Test button in the connection dialog (previously required clicking OK)
  • Fix: Both SonarQube and Jira tokens now coexist in PasswordSafe (previously the second token overwrote the first on Windows Credential Manager)
  • Fix: SonarQube connection test now uses HttpURLConnection so IDE proxy settings are honoured; also checks {"valid":false} response body
3.0.0
  • New: direct Jira ticket creation via Jira REST API v3
  • New: dynamic project and issue-type picker (fields loaded from Jira createmeta)
  • New: deduplication via local mapping file (.sonar-jira-map_<key>.json)
  • New: Jira token stored securely in IntelliJ PasswordSafe
  • New: support for Jira Cloud (Basic auth) and Jira Server/DC (Bearer PAT)
2.0.0
  • Redesigned UI with section headers and inline token validation
  • SonarQube token moved to IntelliJ PasswordSafe
  • Browse projects dialog
  • Incremental file export with issue-key deduplication
1.0.0 — Initial release.

action.description: Open the SonarQube Jira Issuer tool window
